mentions.note: Food company at new lows; market hasn't caught onto benefits of Helmets acquisition from Unilever.
CEO Foley given free pass on Unilever Hellmann's acquisition cost; deal price questioned by Street; should have challenged synergy assumptions vs. packaged food headwinds.

Down 42% over 5 years, worst March in 46 years (29% decline); Unilever deal transforms scale/global reach ($7B to $20B+) and shelf power in flavor category; GLP-1 tailwind (sells taste not calories); $600M synergies by year 3; risks priced in, finally compelling.
Unilever food deal (Hellmann's, Colman's, Knorr) accretive and strategic but ignored in growth market; 16x earnings, 3.81% yield, trades at discount to intrinsic value.

Reports Tuesday; spice/flavor stock very tough to own, down 22% for year; only way out is merger with Unilever's food business (Hellman's, Coleman's); merger would create cost synergies; love combo if announced.
Pressed multiple due to overseas growth; uncertain if capable of beating numbers; packaged food sector is 'new coal' — weak group.
